---
description: "Required disclaimer language for CrewPilot security and threat-model artifacts"
applyTo: '**/.crewpilot/**/threat-model*.md, **/.crewpilot/**/vulnerability-scan*.md, **/.crewpilot/**/security-plan*.md'
---

# Disclaimer Language

CrewPilot's `assure-threat-model` and `assure-vulnerability-scan` skills produce assistive output, not security sign-off. Every artifact matched by this glob includes the disclaimer below as the first content block after the H1 heading.

## Threat model and security plan

Use this verbatim block at the top of any `threat-model.md` or `security-plan.md`:

```markdown
> [!CAUTION]
> **Disclaimer:** This artifact is produced by an assistive tool. It does not provide legal, regulatory, or compliance advice and does not replace professional security review boards, penetration testing teams, compliance auditors, legal counsel, or other qualified human reviewers. The contents are suggested actions and considerations to support a user's own internal security review and decision-making. All threat models, security models, risk classifications, and mitigation recommendations generated by this tool must be independently reviewed and validated by appropriate security and compliance reviewers before use. Output from this tool does not constitute security approval, compliance certification, or regulatory sign-off.
```

## Vulnerability scan

Use this verbatim block at the top of any `vulnerability-scan.md`:

```markdown
> [!CAUTION]
> **Disclaimer:** This vulnerability assessment is produced by an assistive tool. It does not replace professional penetration testing, accredited vulnerability scanners, security review boards, or qualified human reviewers. Findings, severity ratings, and remediation suggestions must be independently validated. Output from this tool does not constitute a security audit, certification, or regulatory sign-off.
```

## Placement rules

* The disclaimer block is the first content after the H1 heading. Nothing else (no metadata, no summary) precedes it.
* Reproduce the text verbatim. Do not paraphrase, summarize, or translate.
* Do not duplicate the disclaimer within the same document. One block per artifact.
* Apply the same block to any derived artifact (executive summary, exported PDF, board issue draft) that contains the assessment content.

## Why this is auto-applied

Centralizing the disclaimer here means a single edit propagates to every future artifact. Skills must not hard-code disclaimer text in their output templates — they reference this instruction file by reading the matching content from the rendered artifact.

> Brought to you by CrewPilot.
